senses.definition: A male who makes or sells sets, or a person who is organized or careful in arranging items into sets (rare or archaic usage).
examples: The old catalog listed a setsman who specialized in arranging antique trinkets into neat sets.

senses.definition: A settlement or place where people establish residence (archaic or dialectal).
examples: They built a sett near the river to live and work.
The sett slowly grew into a village.

senses.definition: Able to be set or arranged to a desired state or value.
examples: This device has a settable timer.
Choose a settable time for the alarm.

senses.definition: An archaic or regional form of a name or term; may refer to a proper noun or surname in historical texts.
examples: The manuscript mentions a place called Settains or Settains.
Findings included references to a sett aine in old records.

senses.definition: The 18th century in Italian; used in English to reference the period or style of the 1700s (often in art or music discussions).
examples: Baroque ornamentation remained popular into the settecento.
The painting reflects settecento aesthetics.

senses.definition: A long upholstered seat for more than one person, with a back and usually arms.
examples: We sat on the settee in the living room.
The new settee is very comfortable.

senses.definition: Plural of settee; multiple long upholstered seats.
examples: The showroom had several settees arranged in a lounge area.

senses.definition: A person or thing that sets or arranges something; in dogs, a setter is a breed that points game.
examples: The dog is a setter trained for pointing birds.
A setter sets the pace during the game.

senses.definition: Past tense of 'set' in some dialectal forms; not common in modern standard English.
examples: He settered the project table yesterday in that old house.

senses.definition: A type of grass used for setting or sealing in horticultural terms; often related to bristly grass used to set turf.
examples: The garden included settergrass along the border.

senses.definition: A dialect or archaic form related to a setting or arrangement; not common in modern usage.
examples: In old texts, settering appears as a variant spelling.

senses.definition: Plural of setter; people or dogs that set or arrange things; also a breed of hunting dog (German Shorthaired is a subset).
examples: The training class welcomed several setters.
setters are often used for pointing game.

senses.definition: A plant known as setterwort; used in old herbal medicine or as a folklore name.
examples: Herbalists mentioned setterwort as a treatment in ancient manuscripts.

senses.definition: Plural of setterwort; various historical references to the plant in herbal contexts.
examples: Old texts list setterworts among medicinal herbs.

senses.definition: The seventh note in a musical scale in some languages; used in chant or theoretical music discussions.
examples: The melody reached settima as the scale progressed.

senses.definition: In Italian, the seventh; used in musical or ordinal contexts in English discussions of Italian terms.
examples: The settimo chord is rarely used outside classical analysis.

senses.definition: The place or environment where something happens; the surroundings or context.
examples: The setting of the story is a small coastal town.
We chose a quiet setting for the meeting.

senses.definition: The act of placing or arranging something in a particular position.
examples: The setting of the jewels on the display table was meticulous.

senses.definition: To put or place something somewhere; to adjust to a desired position.
examples: She setting the vase on the shelf carefully.
Set the table by setting the plates in a neat arrangement.

senses.definition: Multiple environments or contexts; also, options supporting configuration in devices or software.
examples: Change the display settings on your phone.
The novel's settings shift from day to night.

senses.definition: To make a place quiet and calm; to come to rest or to resolve a dispute, debt, or disagreement.
examples: After the argument, they tried to settle their differences.
The dust began to settle after the wind calmed.
They settled in a small village by the lake.

senses.definition: The quality of being able to be settled or decided; the degree to which something can be resolved or stabilized.
examples: The settleability of the agreement was unclear at first.

senses.definition: Capable of being settled or resolved; able to be concluded.
examples: The case is not yet settleable in court.

senses.definition: Calm and comfortable; established or resolved.
examples: They live a settled life in the countryside.
The dust has settled after the storm.

senses.definition: In a settled manner; with calmness or resolve.
examples: He spoke settledly, choosing his words carefully.

senses.definition: The quality of being settled or stable; calmness or composure.
examples: Her settledness helped the team stay focused.

senses.definition: Plural of settledness; multiple states of calmness or stability.
examples: The study compared different settlednesses in participants.
